prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**), computed with PySCF (dft backend). **Gas phase** (no solvent; `solvent: null`). **Charge 0, multiplicity 1** (neutral singlet parent). The finite-difference Fukui indices were built from three SCF states of the fixed input geometry: the neutral (N), the cation N−1 (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and the anion N+1 (charge −1, multiplicity 2), using **Mulliken (PySCF) partial charges**. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals; the default). This was a set of single-point energies on the supplied geometry — the geometry was NOT relaxed at this (or any) level.

**Geometry provenance:** user-supplied `.xyz` at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz` (14 atoms: C7H6O benzaldehyde), used as-is.

**Headline results (dimensionless condensed Fukui indices):**
- **Most electrophilic site (f⁺, susceptibility to nucleophilic attack):** atom index 3 (1-based), a ring carbon, **f⁺ = 0.39939**.
- Most nucleophilic site (f⁻, susceptibility to electrophilic attack): atom index 1 (1-based), the carbonyl **O**, f⁻ = 0.23840.

Per-atom values are available in the full result JSON (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and the dual descriptor for all 14 atoms).

**Convergence / integrity:** The engine's integrity gate reports `status: ok`, **`trustworthy: true`**. All three integrity checks passed: fukui_charge_conservation_f+ (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000), fukui_charge_conservation_f- (sum(f⁻) = 1.0000), and schema_header (common header present) — indicating the N±1 states converged and charge was conserved.

**Warnings:** The result JSON contains no `warnings` array — no engine warnings were reported. (The three integrity `checks` all reported `ok: true`; their `detail` strings are descriptive pass messages, not warnings.)

**Reproducibility:**
- Command: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults <benzaldehyde.xyz> --out <live.json>`
- Result JSON written to `benzaldehyde_fukui_dft.json`; per-atom bar chart written to `.../20260704-175616_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
- Note: `--accept-defaults` was passed to consent to the gas-phase default; the functional, basis, and tier were all set explicitly.

No experimental or literature comparison is offered — ask if you want one and I will verify a source first.
